$comment: Pinned, SHA-verified tsgo (native TypeScript / microsoft/typescript-go) prebuilt channel for the M3 TS SEMANTIC typecheck (src/runtime/ts/, issue #85 — the 2b-i follow-up; R-LANG-002/004 / R-CLI-008). SINGLE source of truth for the pins — parsed by tools/fetch_tsc.py (download + verify-before-use + extract the whole lib/ member dir). src/runtime/ts/CMakeLists.txt forwards this file's PATH to fetch_tsc.py (it does NOT parse the pins itself, so they are never duplicated) and resolves the extracted binary name (tsgo / tsgo.exe) by the platform naming convention. Channel = the TypeScript team's OWN per-platform npm packages (@typescript/native-preview-<platform>), the canonical publisher artifacts — the SAME SHA-pinned third-party build-tool precedent as tools/ts-toolchain.json (esbuild) / tools/v8-prebuilt.json / wgpu-native / CEF codified in docs/signing.md: third-party build tools are authenticated by their OWN publisher via TLS + SHA-pin + verify-before-use, and are explicitly OUT of scope for the engine R-SEC-009 first-party trust root (do NOT route these through tools/verify_artifact.py). tsgo is a standalone native Go binary (a build-TIME semantic typechecker driven with --noEmit; NOT linked into the shipped engine — like esbuild, invoked as a subprocess), so unlike the V8 prebuilt it builds/runs on EVERY toolchain including the local Ninja+Strawberry-GCC Windows dev gate. esbuild deliberately does NOT typecheck (it transpiles + strips types); tsgo is the tsc-class tool that CLOSES the agent author->typecheck->fix loop, surfacing its diagnostics through the R-CLI-008 catalog under the ts.type_error code. UNLIKE esbuild's single-binary member, each tsgo per-platform package ships the binary (package/lib/tsgo[.exe]) ALONGSIDE its default lib.*.d.ts type library in package/lib/ — tsgo resolves those libs RELATIVE TO ITS OWN executable dir and PANICS if they are missing ('bundled: lib.d.ts does not exist; this executable may be misplaced'), so fetch_tsc.py stages the WHOLE package/lib/ dir (member_dir), not one file. VERSION-COHERENCE (every per-platform package carries the SAME tsc_version) is the critical correctness invariant — fetch_tsc.py asserts it at runtime (fail-closed) and tools/tests/test_fetch_tsc.py asserts it statically. tsgo (@typescript/native-preview) is a dev/preview channel (TypeScript 7 / typescript-go), so the version is date-stamped; the EXACT-version SHA pin makes it fully reproducible regardless. License: Apache-2.0 (recorded in tools/license-allowlist.json for provenance, the same build-tool clearance treatment as esbuild).
